text.value: Laboratory Report
Laboratory studies

tr.td: Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Erythrocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field
Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field

Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopy
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field
Bacteria identified in Urine by Culture

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The findings indicate a urinary tract infection (UTI) with significant pyuria, bacteriuria, positive nitrites, and hematuria, confirmed by >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli in urine culture. The presence of glucose in urine may suggest hyperglycemia or impaired renal threshold. Low urine specific gravity could indicate dilute urine. Recommend clinical correlation and appropriate antimicrobial therapy.

text.value: Kristina Pocius

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
